nodes.description: ADPKD includes occasional systemic vascular structural abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: HP:0011004 | Abnormal systemic arterial morphology | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Abnormal urinary electrolyte handling is a frequent laboratory feature of ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012591 | Abnormal urinary electrolyte concentration | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Albuminuria is a frequent urinary abnormality in ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012592 | Albuminuria | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Aortic root aneurysm is an occasional vascular manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002616 | Aortic root aneurysm | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Arachnoid cysts are occasional extrarenal cystic manifestations.

evidence.snippet: HP:0100702 | Arachnoid cyst | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Calcium oxalate stones are another reported nephrolithiasis subtype.

evidence.snippet: HP:0008672 | Calcium oxalate nephrolithiasis | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Intracranial aneurysm and related cerebral arterial dilatation are important ADPKD vascular complications.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004944 | Dilatation of the cerebral artery | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: approximately 9% to 14% develop intracranial aneurysms

nodes.description: Progressive renal cyst burden produces chronic kidney disease.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012622 | Chronic kidney disease | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: When polycystins are absent or under-dosed while cilia remain intact, a cilium-dependent signal that promotes cyst growth is no longer suppressed. This explains why cyst expansion depends on both polycystin loss and the persistence of intact ciliary signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: PCs provide baseline tonic inhibition of a cilium-dependent signal that promotes cyst growth when unchecked following inactivation of PCs.

nodes.description: Declining glomerular filtration rate is a key measurable manifestation of ADPKD progression.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012213 | Decreased glomerular filtration rate | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Management includes strict blood pressure targets and dietary sodium restriction to reduce cardiovascular and kidney-progression risk.
nodes.id: Dietary sodium restriction and blood pressure targets

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Optimal management of ADPKD includes systolic blood pressure lower than 120 mm Hg for most patients

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: dietary sodium restriction (<2000 mg/d)

nodes.description: Creatinine rises as kidney function declines.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003259 | Elevated circulating creatinine concentration | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Progressive cyst burden can cause radiographic or palpable kidney enlargement.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000105 | Enlarged kidney | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Flank or abdominal pain may reflect kidney enlargement, cyst hemorrhage, stones, or infection.

evidence.snippet: HP:0030157 | Flank pain | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hematuria occurs in ADPKD, often related to cyst hemorrhage, stones, or infection.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000790 | Hematuria | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hepatic cysts are the most frequent extrarenal cystic manifestation of ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001407 | Hepatic cysts | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: More than 90% of patients older than 35 years have hepatic cysts

nodes.description: Hypertension is common and is a major modifiable risk factor in ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000822 | Hypertension | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Hypertension affects 70% to 80% of patients with ADPKD

nodes.description: Mitral valve prolapse is a recognized occasional cardiovascular manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001634 | Mitral valve prolapse | Occasional (29-5%)
PKD1 | polycystin 1, transient receptor potential channel interacting | hgnc:9008 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: primarily caused by pathogenic variants in the PKD1 (78%) or PKD2 (15%) genes.

evidence.reference_title: PKD1 / autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: PKD1 | HGNC:9008 | autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease | MONDO:0004691 | AD | Definitive
PKD2 | polycystin 2, transient receptor potential cation channel | hgnc:9009 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: primarily caused by pathogenic variants in the PKD1 (78%) or PKD2 (15%) genes.

evidence.reference_title: PKD2 / autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: PKD2 | HGNC:9009 | autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease | MONDO:0004691 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Pancreatic cysts are an occasional extrarenal cystic feature.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001737 | Pancreatic cysts | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Ongoing cyst expansion, fibrosis, and extracellular-matrix accumulation progressively reduce glomerular filtration, producing chronic kidney disease, elevated creatinine, and eventual kidney failure in many patients.
nodes.id: Parenchymal destruction and progressive renal insufficiency

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: As the disease progresses, this leads to destruction of the normal renal parenchyma, massive renal enlargement, deterioration of renal function, and eventually renal failure in >50% of affected individuals by late adulthood

nodes.description: Orphanet records pituitary growth hormone cell adenoma as a very rare association.

evidence.snippet: HP:0011760 | Pituitary growth hormone cell adenoma | Very rare (<4-1%)

nodes.description: Some patients develop a broader polycystic liver disease phenotype beyond isolated hepatic cysts.

evidence.snippet: HP:0006557 | Polycystic liver disease | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: The proximal defect is reduced activity or loss of the polycystin-1 and polycystin-2 complex in kidney epithelial primary cilia. PKD1 encodes polycystin-1 and PKD2 encodes polycystin-2; pathogenic variants or reduced polycystin dosage disturb the receptor-channel complex that normally helps maintain tubular architecture.
nodes.id: Polycystin complex dosage loss at the renal primary cilium

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: PC1 forms a complex with PC2 via their respective carboxy-terminal tails. Both proteins are expressed in the primary cilia. Mutations in either gene affect the normal architecture of renal tubules, giving rise to ADPKD.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: ADPKD is typically diagnosed in individuals aged 27 to 42 years and is primarily caused by pathogenic variants in the PKD1 (78%) or PKD2 (15%) genes.

nodes.description: Pyelonephritis can complicate ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012330 | Pyelonephritis | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Urinary tract infections occur in a subset of patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000010 | Recurrent urinary tract infections | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Polycystin dysfunction reduces cellular calcium signaling and secondarily increases intracellular cAMP through altered adenylate cyclase and phosphodiesterase activity. Elevated cAMP activates protein kinase A and promotes both epithelial proliferation and chloride-driven fluid secretion.
nodes.id: Reduced calcium and increased cAMP signaling

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: This in turn results in increased adenylate cyclase and decreased phosphodiesterase activity, leading to increased levels of intracellular cAMP.

nodes.description: Reduced sperm motility is an occasional reproductive manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012207 | Reduced sperm motility | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Progressive growth of numerous renal cysts increases total kidney volume and physically replaces or compresses functional kidney parenchyma.
nodes.id: Renal cyst expansion and kidney enlargement

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Patients with MIC 1C to MIC 1E have larger kidneys because of more rapid growth (6%-10% per year)

nodes.description: Fluid-filled renal epithelial cysts are the defining structural feature of ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000107 | Renal cyst | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Cyst-lining epithelial cells proliferate excessively and secrete chloride and fluid into the cyst lumen, expanding epithelial-lined cysts and remodeling surrounding kidney tissue.
nodes.id: Renal epithelial proliferation and fluid secretion

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: ADPKD results from excessive proliferation of renal tubular epithelial cells and remodeling of surrounding structures, giving rise to growth of epithelial-lined cysts accompanied by fibrosis and accumulation of extracellular matrix.

nodes.description: Progressive cyst growth and parenchymal destruction reduce renal function.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000083 | Renal insufficiency | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: A substantial fraction of patients progress to end-stage kidney disease.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003774 | Stage 5 chronic kidney disease | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Approximately 50% of individuals with ADPKD require kidney replacement therapy by 62 years of age.

nodes.description: Tolvaptan is disease-modifying pharmacotherapy for adults at high risk of rapid progression; by antagonizing vasopressin V2 signaling it reduces the annual rate of kidney-volume growth and slows kidney-function decline.
nodes.id: Tolvaptan vasopressin V2 receptor antagonist therapy

evidence.reference_title: Tolvaptan in patients with autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: Tolvaptan, as compared with placebo, slowed the increase in total kidney volume and the decline in kidney function over a 3-year period in patients with ADPKD

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease: A Review.
evidence.snippet: The vasopressin type 2 receptor antagonist tolvaptan reduces the annual rate of eGFR decline by 0.98 to 1.27 mL/min/1.73 m2

nodes.description: Uric acid kidney stones occur in a subset of affected individuals.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000791 | Uric acid nephrolithiasis | Occasional (29-5%)
